front: Installation: a map of the United States made from approximately 300 CRT television screens and neon tubing, with scenes from The Wizard of Oz playing on the Kansas section
back: Electronic Superhighway: Continental U.S., Alaska, Hawaii (Nam June Paik)
work: Electronic Superhighway: Continental U.S., Alaska, Hawaii (1995)

front: Artist: coined the term 'electronic superhighway' in a 1974 report called 'Media Planning for the Postindustrial Society'
work: Electronic Superhighway: Continental U.S., Alaska, Hawaii (1995)

front: Installation: a Buddha statue placed before a closed-circuit television that records and displays its own live image
Installation: a playable cello constructed from stacked television sets, performed by cellist Charlotte Moorman
work: TV Cello / TV Bra for Living Sculpture (collaborations with Charlotte Moorman)

front: Installation: a wearable piece whose 'cups' are small television screens, worn by Charlotte Moorman during performances
back: TV Bra for Living Sculpture (Nam June Paik)
work: TV Cello / TV Bra for Living Sculpture (collaborations with Charlotte Moorman)

front: Artist: Charlotte Moorman served as this artist's frequent collaborator and assistant
work: TV Cello / TV Bra for Living Sculpture (collaborations with Charlotte Moorman)

front: Installation: live satellite broadcast on New Year's Day 1984, hosted by George Plimpton, with Merce Cunningham dancing with time-delayed images of himself
back: Good Morning, Mr. Orwell (Nam June Paik)

front: Installation: Thompson Twins performed 'Hold Me Now'; Laurie Anderson and Peter Gabriel performed the title song
back: Good Morning, Mr. Orwell (Nam June Paik)

front: Installation: problems with a satellite link to France disrupted a planned 'space yodel' for this New Year's 1984 broadcast
back: Good Morning, Mr. Orwell (Nam June Paik)

front: Artist: 'Danger Music' piece instructs the performer to crawl into the womb of a living whale
Artist: 'Playable Piece' instructs the performer to 'cut your left forearm a distance of ten centimeters'; performed by composer Joseph Byrd
Artist: leapt off a stage to cut the necktie of John Cage
Artist: placed magnets on top of television sets to generate abstract distorted forms
Artist: had a breakthrough moment filming Pope Paul VI's arrival in New York City with a newly purchased Sony Portapak
Artist: wife was artist Shigeko Kubota, who created documentary and sculptural homages to Marcel Duchamp
